questions.stem: Which of the following is a governmental fund type?
questions.explanation: The five governmental fund types are: General Fund, Special Revenue Funds, Capital Projects Funds, Debt Service Funds, and Permanent Funds. Enterprise and Internal Service funds are proprietary funds. Pension trust funds are fiduciary funds. Governmental funds use the modified accrual basis and current financial resources measurement focus.
questions.explanationJa: 政府基金（governmental funds）は5種類：一般基金、特別歳入基金、資本プロジェクト基金、債務返済基金、永久基金。企業基金・内部サービス基金は事業活動基金（proprietary funds）、年金信託基金は受託基金（fiduciary funds）に分類される。政府基金は修正発生主義と当期財務資源測定重点を使用する。

questions.stem: Governmental funds use which measurement focus and basis of accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Economic resources measurement focus and accrual basis

choices.label: B
choices.text: Current financial resources measurement focus and modified accrual basis

choices.label: D
choices.text: Economic resources measurement focus and modified accrual basis

questions.explanation: Governmental funds (General, Special Revenue, Capital Projects, Debt Service, Permanent) use the current financial resources measurement focus and modified accrual basis. Under modified accrual, revenues are recognized when measurable and available; expenditures are recognized when the liability is incurred (with certain exceptions for long-term debt principal).
questions.explanationJa: 政府基金は当期財務資源測定重点（current financial resources）と修正発生主義（modified accrual）を使用する。収入は測定可能かつ利用可能な時点で認識し、支出は負債発生時に認識する（長期債務元本は一部例外あり）。

questions.stem: Government-wide financial statements report activities using which basis?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Full accrual basis with economic resources measurement focus

questions.explanation: GASB Statement 34 requires government-wide financial statements (Statement of Net Position and Statement of Activities) to use the economic resources measurement focus and full accrual basis of accounting—similar to private-sector accounting. This includes reporting long-term assets (capital assets with depreciation) and long-term liabilities.
questions.explanationJa: 政府全体の財務諸表（純資産計算書・活動計算書）は、経済的資源測定重点と完全発生主義を使用する。民間企業の会計と同様であり、長期資産（減価償却付き資本資産）と長期負債を報告する。

questions.stem: When a government records the adoption of its annual budget in the general fund, what entry is made for estimated revenues exceeding appropriations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Estimated Revenues; Credit Appropriations and Budgetary Fund Balance

questions.explanation: When a government adopts its budget: Debit Estimated Revenues (for the total expected revenues), Credit Appropriations (for authorized spending), and the difference is credited to Budgetary Fund Balance (if revenues exceed appropriations, indicating a planned surplus). These are budgetary accounts that are nominal and closed at year-end.
questions.explanationJa: 予算採択時の仕訳：借方に見積歳入（予想歳入総額）、貸方に歳出予算額（承認支出額）を記入する。歳入が歳出を超える差額は予算基金残高の貸方に計上する（計画的黒字）。これらは名目的な予算勘定であり、年度末に締め切る。

questions.stem: A city issues a purchase order for $50,000 of supplies. What is the journal entry at the time of the purchase order?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Encumbrances $50,000; Credit Budgetary Fund Balance Reserved for Encumbrances $50,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry until supplies are received

questions.explanation: In governmental fund accounting, when a purchase order or contract is issued, the estimated commitment is recorded as an encumbrance. The entry reserves a portion of the fund balance to reflect the outstanding commitment. When the goods/services are received, the encumbrance entry is reversed and an expenditure is recorded.
questions.explanationJa: 購入注文書や契約の発行時に、見積額をエンカンブランス（債務負担行為）として記録し、基金残高の一部を留保する。物品・サービス受領時にエンカンブランスを取消し、実際の支出を計上する。

questions.stem: Infrastructure assets (roads, bridges) of a government are reported in:

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both governmental fund and government-wide statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neither statement; they are disclosed in notes only

questions.explanation: Capital assets (including infrastructure) are long-term assets reported in the government-wide financial statements under the economic resources measurement focus. They are NOT reported in governmental fund statements because those use the current financial resources measurement focus (only current assets and liabilities). The cost of acquiring capital assets is reported as a capital expenditure in the fund statements.
questions.explanationJa: 資本資産（インフラ含む）は政府全体の財務諸表でのみ報告される長期資産である。政府基金財務諸表は当期財務資源測定重点（流動資産・負債のみ）を使用するため、資本資産は計上しない。基金財務諸表では取得費用を資本支出として報告する。

questions.stem: Under GASB 54, which fund balance classification represents amounts that can be spent only for specific purposes imposed by external resource providers or enabling legislation?
questions.explanation: GASB 54 establishes a hierarchy of fund balance classifications: Nonspendable (not in spendable form or legally required to remain intact), Restricted (constrained by external parties or enabling legislation), Committed (constrained by the government's highest decision-making authority), Assigned (intended for specific purposes by authorized body), and Unassigned (residual for general fund).
questions.explanationJa: GASB 54による基金残高の分類階層：Nonspendable（支出不能形態または法的維持義務）、Restricted（外部制約または授権法令）、Committed（最高意思決定機関による制約）、Assigned（権限機関による目的指定）、Unassigned（一般基金の残余）。

questions.stem: Under GASB 87 (Leases), a lessee government should recognize for a lease with a term of more than 12 months:

choices.label: A
choices.text: An operating expense on a straight-line basis only

choices.label: B
choices.text: A lease liability and an intangible right-to-use lease asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: A capital lease asset and obligation only for finance leases

questions.explanation: GASB 87 requires lessees to recognize a lease liability and an intangible right-to-use lease asset for virtually all leases with terms greater than 12 months. Unlike ASC 842, GASB 87 uses a single model (similar to a finance lease) for all leases—there is no operating/finance lease classification distinction for lessees.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 87では、12か月超のほぼ全てのリースについて、リース負債と無形の使用権資産を認識する。ASC 842と異なり、GASB 87は単一モデル（ファイナンスリース類似）を使用し、借手にとってオペレーティング/ファイナンスの分類区別はない。

questions.stem: In government-wide financial statements, net position is categorized into which three components?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Invested in capital assets, restricted, unrestricted

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net investment in capital assets, restricted, unrestricted

questions.explanation: GASB Statement 34 (as amended by GASB 63) classifies net position in government-wide statements into three categories: (1) Net investment in capital assets (capital assets less accumulated depreciation and related debt), (2) Restricted (by external constraints or enabling legislation), and (3) Unrestricted (the residual).
questions.explanationJa: 政府全体の財務諸表における純資産は3区分である：(1) Net investment in capital assets（資本資産 - 減価償却累計額 - 関連債務）、(2) Restricted（外部制約または授権法令）、(3) Unrestricted（残余）。

questions.stem: A transfer from the General Fund to the Debt Service Fund to make a bond payment is reported as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: An expenditure in the General Fund and a revenue in the Debt Service Fund

choices.label: B
choices.text: A transfer out in the General Fund and a transfer in in the Debt Service Fund (reported separately from revenues and expenditures)

questions.explanation: GASB Codification 1800 requires that interfund transfers be reported separately from revenues and expenditures. The transferring fund reports 'Other Financing Uses—Transfer Out' and the receiving fund reports 'Other Financing Sources—Transfer In.' These are not revenues or expenditures because no exchange has occurred with an outside party.
questions.explanationJa: 基金間振替は歳入・歳出とは別に報告する。振替元は「Other Financing Uses - Transfer Out」、受取側は「Other Financing Sources - Transfer In」として表示する。外部との交換取引ではないため、歳入・歳出には該当しない。

questions.stem: A city levies property taxes of $10,000,000 for the current fiscal year. It estimates 2% will be uncollectible and 5% will be collected more than 60 days after year-end (the city's availability period). What amount of property tax revenue should the city recognize in the current year under modified accrual?
questions.explanation: Under modified accrual, revenue is recognized when measurable and available (collectible within the availability period). Revenue = Levy − Uncollectible − Unavailable = $10,000,000 − ($10,000,000 × 2%) − ($10,000,000 × 5%) = $10,000,000 − $200,000 − $500,000 = $9,300,000. The $500,000 is reported as deferred inflows of resources.
questions.explanationJa: 修正発生主義では、収入は測定可能かつ利用可能（利用可能期間内に回収可能）な時に認識する。収入 = 課税額 - 回収不能額 - 利用不能額 = $10,000,000 - $200,000 - $500,000 = $9,300,000。利用可能期間外の$500,000は繰延資源流入（deferred inflows）として報告する。

questions.stem: A county recorded an encumbrance of $120,000 for a road repair contract. When the work was completed, the actual invoice was $115,000. What is the net effect on expenditures and fund balance reserved for encumbrances?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expenditures increase $115,000; Reserved for encumbrances decreases $120,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expenditures increase $120,000; Reserved for encumbrances decreases $120,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expenditures increase $115,000; Reserved for encumbrances decreases $115,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Expenditures increase $120,000; No change to reserved for encumbrances

questions.explanation: When goods/services are received: (1) Reverse full encumbrance: Debit Budgetary Fund Balance Reserved for Encumbrances $120,000, Credit Encumbrances $120,000. (2) Record actual expenditure: Debit Expenditures $115,000, Credit Vouchers Payable $115,000. Net effect: Expenditures up $115,000, Reserved for Encumbrances down $120,000 (releasing $5,000 back to unreserved fund balance).
questions.explanationJa: 受領時の処理：(1) エンカンブランス全額取消 - 借方 予算基金残高留保 $120,000 / 貸方 エンカンブランス $120,000。(2) 実際支出計上 - 借方 支出 $115,000 / 貸方 未払金 $115,000。純影響：支出$115,000増、留保$120,000減（差額$5,000が未留保基金残高に戻る）。

questions.stem: A city's Debt Service Fund makes a scheduled payment of $500,000 principal and $200,000 interest on general obligation bonds. The fund received a $700,000 transfer from the General Fund. What is the total expenditure reported in the Debt Service Fund?
questions.explanation: In a Debt Service Fund (governmental fund), both principal and interest payments on long-term debt are reported as expenditures when due (matured). Principal expenditure = $500,000 + Interest expenditure = $200,000 = Total expenditures $700,000. The $700,000 transfer in is reported as Other Financing Sources, not revenue.
questions.explanationJa: 債務返済基金（政府基金）では、長期債務の元本・利息は支払期日到来時に支出として報告する。元本支出 $500,000 + 利息支出 $200,000 = 総支出 $700,000。振替収入$700,000はその他財源（OFS）であり歳入ではない。

questions.stem: A Capital Projects Fund issues $5,000,000 of general obligation bonds at 102 (a premium of $100,000). The premium must be transferred to the Debt Service Fund. What amounts are reported in the Capital Projects Fund?
questions.explanation: Bond proceeds (including premium) are reported as Other Financing Sources in the issuing fund: $5,000,000 face + $100,000 premium = $5,100,000 OFS. The required transfer of premium to Debt Service Fund is reported as Other Financing Uses—Transfer Out $100,000 in the Capital Projects Fund.
questions.explanationJa: 社債発行収入（プレミアム含む）は発行基金でOFS（その他財源）として報告：額面$5,000,000 + プレミアム$100,000 = $5,100,000。プレミアムの債務返済基金への振替は、資本プロジェクト基金でOFU（その他財源使途）- 振替支出$100,000として報告する。

questions.stem: A government's General Fund reports capital outlay expenditures of $3,000,000 during the year. Depreciation on governmental capital assets totals $800,000. What is the net adjustment when converting from fund statements to the government-wide Statement of Activities?
questions.explanation: Converting fund to government-wide: Remove capital outlay expenditure (increases net position by $3,000,000 because the expenditure overstated the decrease). Add depreciation expense (decreases net position by $800,000). Net adjustment = +$3,000,000 − $800,000 = +$2,200,000 increase in net position.

questions.stem: A state government receives a $2,000,000 federal grant that must be spent on highway maintenance. As of year-end, the state has spent $1,400,000 on qualifying expenditures and collected $1,200,000 from the federal government. Under modified accrual, what amount of grant revenue does the state recognize?
questions.explanation: This is an expenditure-driven (reimbursement-type) grant. Revenue is recognized when qualifying expenditures are incurred (the eligibility requirement is met) and the amount is available. The state has incurred $1,400,000 in qualifying expenditures, so it recognizes $1,400,000 in revenue. The $200,000 difference ($1,400,000 − $1,200,000 received) is reported as a grant receivable.
questions.explanationJa: 支出主導型（精算型）の補助金では、適格支出の発生により適格要件が充足され、金額が利用可能な時点で収入を認識する。適格支出$1,400,000を認識。差額$200,000（$1,400,000 - 受取済$1,200,000）は補助金受取債権として報告する。

questions.stem: A city's water utility (enterprise fund) has capital assets of $8,000,000 (net of accumulated depreciation $2,000,000), outstanding revenue bonds of $5,000,000 used to finance those assets, restricted assets of $600,000 (bond covenant reserve), and unrestricted net assets of $1,200,000. What is the net investment in capital assets?
questions.explanation: Net investment in capital assets = Capital assets (net) − Related debt. Net capital assets = $8,000,000 (already net of depreciation). Related debt = $5,000,000. Net investment in capital assets = $8,000,000 − $5,000,000 = $3,000,000.
questions.explanationJa: Net investment in capital assets = 資本資産（純額）- 関連債務。$8,000,000（減価償却控除後）- $5,000,000 = $3,000,000。

questions.stem: The General Fund pays $150,000 to the Internal Service Fund for printing services at a rate comparable to outside vendors. How is this reported in each fund?

choices.label: A
choices.text: General Fund: Expenditure $150,000; Internal Service Fund: Revenue $150,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: General Fund: Transfer out $150,000; Internal Service Fund: Transfer in $150,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: General Fund: Expenditure $150,000; Internal Service Fund: no entry (eliminated)

choices.label: D
choices.text: General Fund: no entry; Internal Service Fund: Revenue $150,000

questions.explanation: Exchange-like interfund transactions (quasi-external transactions) are reported as revenues and expenditures/expenses in the respective funds. The General Fund records an expenditure and the Internal Service Fund records revenue, just as if the transaction were with an outside party. These are NOT eliminated in fund-level statements.
questions.explanationJa: 交換取引類似の基金間取引（準外部取引）は、各基金で収入・支出として報告する。一般基金は支出、内部サービス基金は収入を計上する。外部取引と同様の処理であり、基金レベルの財務諸表では消去しない。

questions.stem: A city's governmental funds report debt service expenditures of $2,400,000 ($2,000,000 principal and $400,000 interest). The city also accrued $50,000 of interest payable at year-end (not yet matured). What is the total expense related to this debt on the government-wide Statement of Activities?
questions.explanation: In the government-wide statements (full accrual): Principal repayment ($2,000,000) is NOT an expense—it reduces the bond liability. Interest paid ($400,000) IS an expense. Accrued interest ($50,000) is also recognized as expense under full accrual. Total interest expense = $400,000 + $50,000 = $450,000.
questions.explanationJa: 政府全体の財務諸表（完全発生主義）では、元本返済$2,000,000は費用ではなく負債の減少。支払利息$400,000は費用。未払利息$50,000も発生主義で費用認識。利息費用合計 = $400,000 + $50,000 = $450,000。

questions.stem: A city has a Special Revenue Fund for parks funded by a dedicated 1% sales tax. During the year, the sales tax generated $3,600,000. Of this amount, $3,400,000 was collected by year-end and the remaining $200,000 is expected to be collected within 45 days (the city's availability period is 60 days). What amount of sales tax revenue is recognized in the Special Revenue Fund?
questions.explanation: Under modified accrual, revenue is recognized when measurable and available. Available means collectible within the availability period (60 days for this city). The $3,400,000 collected + $200,000 expected within 45 days (within the 60-day window) = $3,600,000 is all measurable and available. Full amount is recognized as revenue.
questions.explanationJa: 修正発生主義では、収入は測定可能かつ利用可能（利用可能期間内に回収可能）な時に認識する。回収済$3,400,000 + 45日以内に回収見込の$200,000（60日の利用可能期間内）= $3,600,000全額が認識対象である。

questions.stem: Under GASB 84, which of the following is NOT classified as a fiduciary fund?
questions.explanation: Under GASB 84, fiduciary funds include pension (and other employee benefit) trust funds, investment trust funds, private-purpose trust funds, and custodial funds. Internal service funds are proprietary funds, not fiduciary funds. They are used to report activities that provide goods or services to other departments within the government.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 84では、受託基金には年金信託基金、投資信託基金、私的目的信託基金、管理基金が含まれる。内部サービス基金は企業型基金であり受託基金ではない。内部サービス基金は政府内の他部門にサービスを提供する活動に使用される。

questions.stem: A legally separate entity provides services entirely to the primary government. The governing body of the entity is substantively the same as the primary government. Under GASB 14 (as amended), how should this component unit be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a discretely presented component unit

questions.explanation: A component unit should be blended when its governing body is substantively the same as the primary government AND it provides services entirely (or almost entirely) to the primary government. Blended presentation integrates the component unit's financial data with the primary government's funds.
questions.explanationJa: 構成単位は、統治機関が主要政府と実質的に同一であり、かつサービスを全て（またはほぼ全て）主要政府に提供する場合、混合表示する。混合表示では構成単位の財務データが主要政府の基金に統合される。

questions.subtopic: Proprietary Fund - Statement of Net Position
questions.stem: In the statement of net position for an enterprise fund, net position is classified into which three categories?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Fund balance: nonspendable, restricted, and unrestricted

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net investment in capital assets, restricted, and unrestricted

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contributed capital, retained earnings, and reserves

choices.label: D
choices.text: Invested in capital assets, designated, and undesignated

questions.explanation: Under GASB 34, proprietary fund net position is reported in three categories: (1) net investment in capital assets (capital assets less related debt), (2) restricted (externally imposed constraints), and (3) unrestricted. Fund balance categories (nonspendable, restricted, committed, assigned, unassigned) apply to governmental funds under GASB 54.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 34では、企業型基金の純資産を3区分で報告する：(1) 資本資産への純投資額（資本資産から関連負債を控除）、(2) 制限付き（外部制約あり）、(3) 非制限。基金残高区分（使用不能、制限付き、確約済み、割当済み、未割当て）はGASB 54に基づく政府型基金に適用。

questions.stem: A city levies property taxes of $5,000,000 for the fiscal year ending June 30, Year 1. Of this amount, $4,700,000 is collected by June 30, $150,000 is expected within 60 days after year-end, and $150,000 is expected after 60 days. The city's availability period is 60 days. What amount of property tax revenue should the General Fund recognize?
questions.explanation: Under modified accrual (governmental funds), property tax revenue is recognized when measurable and available. Collected amount ($4,700,000) plus amount collectible within the availability period ($150,000 within 60 days) = $4,850,000. The $150,000 expected after 60 days is recorded as deferred inflows of resources.
questions.explanationJa: 修正発生主義（政府型基金）では、固定資産税収入は測定可能かつ利用可能な時に認識する。回収済（$4,700,000）＋利用可能期間内に回収見込（60日以内の$150,000）= $4,850,000。60日後に回収見込の$150,000は繰延収入として計上。

questions.stem: In a General Fund year-end closing entry, which of the following accounts would be DEBITED?
questions.explanation: At year-end, budgetary accounts are closed by reversing the opening budget entry. Since Appropriations was credited at the beginning of the year (as an authorized spending limit), it is debited to close. Estimated Revenues was debited at the beginning of the year, so it is credited to close. Expenditures (an actual account) is also credited to close.
questions.explanationJa: 年度末に予算勘定は期首予算仕訳の逆仕訳で閉鎖される。歳出予算額（Appropriations）は期首に貸方計上されたため、閉鎖時は借方に記入。歳入見積額（Estimated Revenues）は期首に借方計上されたため貸方で閉鎖。実績の支出（Expenditures）も貸方で閉鎖。

questions.stem: Under GASB 68, a state government participating in a single-employer defined benefit pension plan reports a net pension liability of $50 million. Where is this liability reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In the General Fund balance sheet only

choices.label: B
choices.text: In the government-wide statement of net position only

choices.label: C
choices.text: In both the fund-level and government-wide statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: In the notes to the financial statements only

questions.explanation: Under GASB 68, the net pension liability is a long-term obligation. In governmental fund financial statements (modified accrual), long-term liabilities are not reported. The net pension liability appears only in the government-wide statement of net position, which uses full accrual accounting. Enterprise funds (proprietary) also report it if applicable.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 68では、純年金負債は長期債務である。政府型基金財務諸表（修正発生主義）では長期負債は報告しない。純年金負債は完全発生主義を使用する政府全体の純資産計算書にのみ表示される。企業型基金（事業会計）にも該当があれば報告する。

questions.stem: Under GASB 75, which of the following is used to calculate the total OPEB liability?

choices.label: B
choices.text: The rate of return on plan investments for funded plans, potentially blended with a municipal bond rate

questions.explanation: Under GASB 75, the discount rate for calculating the total OPEB liability is the single rate that reflects (1) the long-term expected rate of return on plan investments for the portion of projected benefit payments that are projected to be met by plan assets, and (2) a tax-exempt municipal bond rate (20-year AA/higher) for the portion not projected to be met by plan assets.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 75では、総OPEB負債の割引率は、(1) 制度資産で充足される予測給付支払部分には制度投資の長期期待収益率、(2) 制度資産で充足されない部分には非課税地方債利率（20年物AA格以上）を反映する単一利率を使用する。

questions.stem: A city owes employees $400,000 for accumulated vacation leave. Of this amount, $80,000 is expected to be paid from General Fund resources in the next fiscal year. How should this liability be reported?
questions.explanation: In governmental funds (modified accrual), compensated absences are recognized as a liability only to the extent they are expected to be liquidated with expendable available financial resources (the amount due in the next year = $80,000). In the government-wide statements (full accrual), the entire $400,000 liability is reported.
questions.explanationJa: 政府型基金（修正発生主義）では、有給休暇負債は使用可能な財源で清算される見込額（翌年度支払額 = $80,000）のみ負債認識する。政府全体の財務諸表（完全発生主義）では$400,000全額を負債計上する。

questions.stem: A city issues special assessment bonds for sidewalk improvements in a specific neighborhood. The city is NOT obligated in any manner for the debt. How should the city report this activity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Report in a Capital Projects Fund with the debt in the government-wide statements

choices.label: B
choices.text: Report in an Agency Fund (custodial fund)

questions.explanation: When a government has NO obligation for special assessment debt (not even in a secondary capacity), the construction activity and debt service are reported in a custodial fund (formerly agency fund). The government acts merely as an agent, collecting assessments from property owners and passing them to bondholders.
questions.explanationJa: 政府が特別賦課金債務に対して一切義務を負わない（二次的にも）場合、建設活動と元利償還は管理基金（旧代理基金）で報告する。政府は単に代理人として不動産所有者から賦課金を徴収し債券保有者に渡す役割のみを果たす。

questions.stem: A city's central motor pool provides vehicles to other departments, charging them based on mileage. Which fund type should the motor pool use?
questions.explanation: An Internal Service Fund is a proprietary fund used to report activities that provide goods or services to other departments or agencies of the primary government on a cost-reimbursement basis. A central motor pool providing vehicles to city departments is a classic example. Enterprise funds are used for activities that charge the general public.
questions.explanationJa: 内部サービス基金は、主要政府の他部門に原価償還ベースで商品・サービスを提供する活動を報告する企業型基金である。市の各部門に車両を提供する車両管理プールは典型例。企業基金は一般市民に課金する活動に使用する。

questions.stem: A city receives a donation of $1,000,000 with the stipulation that the principal must be maintained intact and earnings used to maintain a public park. In which fund type should this donation be reported?
questions.explanation: A Permanent Fund is a governmental fund that reports resources legally restricted so that only earnings (not principal) may be used for purposes that benefit the government or its citizens. Since the earnings benefit public purposes (public park maintenance), a Permanent Fund is appropriate. If earnings benefited private individuals, a Private-Purpose Trust Fund would be used.
questions.explanationJa: 永久基金は、法的に制限され元本は保全し運用益のみを政府または市民の利益のために使用する資源を報告する政府型基金。運用益が公的目的（公園維持）に充てられるため永久基金が適切。運用益が私的個人の利益なら私的目的信託基金を使用する。

questions.stem: A city council passes a formal resolution designating $500,000 of General Fund balance for future capital improvements but does not create a legal obligation. Under GASB 54, how should this fund balance be classified?
questions.explanation: Under GASB 54, committed fund balance is constrained by formal action of the government's highest level of decision-making authority (the city council). A formal resolution by the council constitutes such action. The constraint can only be removed by a similar formal action. Committed differs from restricted (externally imposed) and assigned (management intent without formal action).
questions.explanationJa: GASB 54では、確約済基金残高は政府の最高意思決定機関（市議会）の正式な行為によって拘束される。市議会の正式決議はそのような行為に該当する。拘束解除も同様の正式行為を要する。制限付き（外部制約）および割当済み（正式行為のない経営意図）とは異なる。

questions.stem: Under GASB 87, how does a government lessor account for a lease?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a lease receivable and a deferred inflow of resources

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize a lease receivable and lease revenue immediately

choices.label: C
choices.text: Remove the leased asset and recognize a gain or loss

choices.label: D
choices.text: Continue reporting the asset and recognize rent revenue as received

questions.explanation: Under GASB 87, the lessor recognizes a lease receivable (for future lease payments) and a deferred inflow of resources at lease commencement. The deferred inflow is recognized as revenue (lease revenue) over the lease term in a systematic and rational manner. The leased asset remains on the lessor's books and continues to be depreciated.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 87では、貸手はリース開始時にリース債権（将来のリース料）と繰延収入を認識する。繰延収入はリース期間にわたり体系的かつ合理的にリース収入として認識する。リース資産は貸手の帳簿に残り減価償却を継続する。

questions.stem: In the government-wide Statement of Activities, program revenues are classified into which categories?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Charges for services, operating grants and contributions, and capital grants and contributions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Tax revenues, intergovernmental revenues, and miscellaneous revenues

questions.explanation: Under GASB 34, the Statement of Activities presents expenses by function/program, offset by three categories of program revenues: (1) charges for services, (2) operating grants and contributions, and (3) capital grants and contributions. The net expense or revenue for each function is then supported by general revenues (taxes, unrestricted grants, etc.).
questions.explanationJa: GASB 34では、活動計算書は機能・プログラム別の費用を3区分のプログラム収入で相殺する：(1) サービス対価、(2) 経常補助金・寄附、(3) 資本的補助金・寄附。各機能の純費用または純収入は一般収入（税収、非制限補助金等）で補填される。

questions.subtopic: Derived Tax Revenue vs Imposed Nonexchange Revenue
questions.stem: Which of the following is an example of a derived tax revenue transaction?
questions.explanation: Under GASB 33, derived tax revenues result from assessments imposed on exchange transactions (e.g., income taxes, sales taxes). Property taxes are imposed nonexchange revenues (assessed on property ownership, not an exchange). Federal grants with eligibility requirements are government-mandated or voluntary nonexchange transactions. Fines and penalties are imposed nonexchange revenues.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 33では、派生的税収は交換取引に課される評価から生じる（例：所得税、売上税）。固定資産税は賦課型非交換収入（不動産所有に課される）。適格要件付き連邦補助金は政府命令型または自発的非交換取引。罰金・科料は賦課型非交換収入。

questions.stem: In the government-wide financial statements, which of the following capital assets are NOT depreciated?
questions.explanation: Under GASB 34, capital assets in the government-wide statements are depreciated except for land, inexhaustible works of art/historical treasures, and construction in progress (CIP). CIP is not depreciated because it is not yet placed in service. Once complete, it is reclassified to the appropriate depreciable category.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 34では、政府全体の財務諸表の資本資産は、土地、消耗しない芸術作品・歴史的財産、建設仮勘定（CIP）を除き減価償却する。CIPはまだ使用開始されていないため償却しない。完成後は適切な償却区分に振り替える。

questions.stem: A city issues $10,000,000 of general obligation bonds at 102 in its Capital Projects Fund. How should the $200,000 premium be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As revenue in the Capital Projects Fund

choices.label: B
choices.text: As an other financing source in the Capital Projects Fund

choices.label: C
choices.text: Transferred to the Debt Service Fund as an other financing source

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amortized over the life of the bonds in the Capital Projects Fund

questions.explanation: Bond premiums received are typically transferred to the Debt Service Fund to be used for debt service payments. In governmental funds, the bond proceeds (at par) are reported as an other financing source in the Capital Projects Fund, and the premium is transferred to the Debt Service Fund (other financing source). Premiums are not amortized in governmental fund statements.
questions.explanationJa: 債券プレミアムは通常、元利償還に充てるため債務償還基金に移管される。政府型基金では、額面の債券収入は資本プロジェクト基金のその他財源として報告し、プレミアムは債務償還基金に移管（その他財源）する。政府型基金の財務諸表ではプレミアムは償却しない。

questions.stem: Which of the following correctly describes the difference between expenditures (governmental funds) and expenses (government-wide statements)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both are recognized on the accrual basis

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expenditures include capital outlay and debt principal payments; expenses include depreciation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expenditures include depreciation; expenses include capital outlay

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is no difference; the terms are interchangeable

questions.explanation: In governmental funds (modified accrual), expenditures include capital outlays (full cost when acquired) and debt principal payments. In government-wide statements (full accrual), expenses include depreciation on capital assets and interest expense on an accrual basis. Capital outlays are capitalized as assets, and principal payments reduce the liability rather than being expensed.
questions.explanationJa: 政府型基金（修正発生主義）では、支出に資本的支出（取得時全額）と債務元本返済が含まれる。政府全体の財務諸表（完全発生主義）では、費用に資本資産の減価償却と発生ベースの利息費用が含まれる。資本的支出は資産計上され、元本返済は負債を減少させる。

questions.stem: Under the modified approach for reporting infrastructure assets (GASB 34), which of the following is true?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Infrastructure assets are depreciated using double-declining balance

choices.label: B
choices.text: Preservation costs are capitalized; no depreciation is recorded

choices.label: C
choices.text: Preservation costs are expensed; no depreciation is recorded

choices.label: D
choices.text: Infrastructure assets are reported at fair value each year

questions.explanation: Under the modified approach, eligible infrastructure assets (that are part of a network or subsystem maintained at a condition level established by the government) are NOT depreciated. Instead, all expenditures for preservation (maintenance to keep assets at the established condition level) are expensed. Only additions and improvements that increase capacity or efficiency are capitalized.
questions.explanationJa: 修正アプローチでは、適格なインフラ資産（政府が設定した状態水準で維持されるネットワークの一部）は減価償却しない。保全に係る全支出（設定した状態水準を維持するための維持費）は費用処理する。容量・効率を向上させる追加・改良のみ資産計上する。

questions.stem: A state government operates an external investment pool for local governments. Under GASB, in which fund type should the state report this pool?
questions.explanation: Under GASB 34, an external investment pool (where the sponsoring government commingles investments of other governments) is reported in an Investment Trust Fund, which is a fiduciary fund type. The external portion of the pool is reported in this fund. The sponsoring government's own portion is reported in the fund that holds the investment.
questions.explanationJa: GASB 34では、外部投資プール（主催政府が他の政府の投資を混合運用）は投資信託基金（受託基金の一種）で報告する。プールの外部部分をこの基金で報告する。主催政府自身の持分は投資を保有する基金で報告する。
